task_completion.description: Create a Pull Request to merge the hotfix into main
task_completion.visual_evidence: Azure DevOps Pull Request creation screen or PR overview showing the source branch as 'hotfix/login-security-patch', the target branch as 'main', and the title exactly 'Fix critical login vulnerability'.

task_completion.description: Complete the PR to main without deleting the source branch
task_completion.visual_evidence: The PR to 'main' is marked as 'Completed'. During the completion dialog, the 'Delete source branch' checkbox must be UNCHECKED, or subsequent screens must show the 'hotfix/login-security-patch' branch still exists.

task_completion.description: Create a Pull Request to merge the hotfix into develop
task_completion.visual_evidence: Azure DevOps Pull Request creation screen or PR overview showing the source branch as 'hotfix/login-security-patch', the target branch as 'develop', and the title exactly 'Sync hotfix to develop'.
The PR to 'develop' is marked as 'Completed' with a green status badge in the Azure DevOps interface.

task_completion.description: Delete the hotfix branch after both merges
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Delete source branch' checkbox is checked during the completion of the second PR, or the agent manually deletes 'hotfix/login-security-patch' from the Branches page (showing a deletion confirmation or the branch missing from the list).

task_completion.description: Verify the fix exists in both main and develop branches
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent navigates to the 'Commits' or 'Files' view in Azure DevOps for BOTH the 'main' and 'develop' branches to visually confirm the hotfix commits or file changes are present.

integrity.description: Agent used the Azure DevOps web interface to perform the tasks
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the Azure DevOps web UI (http://localhost/DefaultCollection) for creating and completing PRs, rather than solely using local git CLI commands.

integrity.description: Agent used Pull Requests instead of direct pushes
integrity.visual_evidence: Changes to 'main' and 'develop' should clearly come from completed Pull Requests in the UI, not from direct terminal 'git push' commands bypassing the PR process.
The agent actually loaded the branch history or file contents in the browser to check for the fix, rather than just typing a verification command that does nothing or hallucinating success.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The actual repository name in the Azure DevOps environment is 'TailwindTraders', not 'TailwindTraders-Auth' as stated in the task description. Do not penalize the agent for operating within the 'TailwindTraders' repository. The Azure DevOps instance is hosted at http://localhost/DefaultCollection/TailwindTraders.
